warnings.message: Some PDF pages have no strong text-window match in the EPUB
Some dense PDF word windows are missing from the EPUB and may indicate sliced sentence interiors
Some first body lines near the top of PDF pages are not found in the EPUB
Some last body lines near the bottom of PDF pages are not found in the EPUB
Some adjacent EPUB paragraphs look like possible faulty line or page breaks
Some chapter headings are long enough to suggest swallowed body text
Some EPUB enumerators look like possible sequence jumps and need triage
Repeated word windows may indicate ghost-layer duplication

The first is that of p. 131.

overlong_heading_candidates.text: II. Having manifested that the saints hold peculiar fellowship with the Lord Jesus, it neatly follows that we show wherein it is that they have this peculiar communion with him.

II. For the knowledge of ourselves, which is the SECOND part of our wisdom, this consists in these three things, which our Savior sends his Spirit to convince the world of, — even "sin, righteousness, and judgement," John 16:8.
II. Christ values his saints, values believers (which is the second branch of that conjugal affection he bears towards them), having taken them into the relation whereof we speak.
I. Communion with Christ in purchased grace, as unto acceptation with God, from the obedience of his life and efficacy of his death, is the first thing we inquire into.

II. Our communion with the Lord Jesus as to that grace of sanctification and purification whereof we have made mention, in the several distinctions and degrees thereof, formerly, is neatly to be considered.
